name: 50-50 Grind
history: Both trucks grind along a rail or ledge simultaneously. One of the most fundamental grind tricks and a cornerstone of street and ledge skating.

name: 5-0 Grind
history: Only the back truck grinds the obstacle while the front is raised. The name comes from the nose being elevated at a '5 o'clock' angle.

name: Nosegrind
history: The front truck grinds on the obstacle while the back truck is elevated. The counterpart to the 5-0 and a staple of ledge and rail skating.

name: Crooked Grind
history: Also called a K-grind. The front truck grinds while the board is angled so the nose points down into or across the obstacle. A highly technical grind.
youtube_query: how to crooked grind skateboard tutorial

name: Salad Grind
history: The back truck grinds while the board is tilted at an angle (frontside). A stylish variation between a 5-0 and a boardslide.
youtube_query: how to salad grind skateboard tutorial

name: Overcrook
history: A crooked grind where the nose points upward past the ledge and the front truck grinds on the far side. The board goes 'over' the obstacle.
youtube_query: how to overcrook grind skateboard tutorial

name: Feeble Grind
history: The back truck grinds while the front truck hangs over on the far side of the obstacle (usually a rail). A fundamental rail trick.
youtube_query: how to feeble grind skateboard tutorial

name: Smith Grind
history: The back truck grinds the obstacle while the front truck hangs below and the board is angled. Named after Mike Smith who invented it in the early 1980s.
youtube_query: how to smith grind skateboard tutorial

name: Willy Grind
history: A grind where only the back truck makes contact and the board is nearly parallel to the obstacle, creating a unique angled position. Named after Willy Santos.

name: Nose Blunt Grind
history: The nose of the board presses against the top of the obstacle while grinding, with the front truck locked on. A difficult stall-grind hybrid.

name: Blunt Grind
history: The tail of the board presses against the obstacle while the back truck grinds. Involves the tail making contact with the top surface.

name: Axle Stall
history: Both trucks come to a complete stop on top of an obstacle, then the skater drops back in. A foundational transition and ledge trick.
youtube_query: how to axle stall skateboard tutorial

name: Nosestall
history: The front truck stalls on the edge of an obstacle. Often used as a setup for other tricks or as a stylish pause in a line.

name: Tailstall
history: The tail of the board stalls on the edge of an obstacle. A simple but effective trick used in transition and street skating.

name: Boardslide
history: The board slides perpendicular to the obstacle (rail or ledge) with the middle of the board on the surface. A fundamental slide trick in street skating.

name: Frontside Boardslide
history: A boardslide where the skater approaches frontside (chest facing the obstacle). Considered slightly more accessible than the backside version.
youtube_query: how to frontside boardslide skateboard tutorial

name: Backside Boardslide
history: A boardslide approached from the backside (back facing the obstacle). Requires blind commitment which makes it trickier to learn.
youtube_query: how to backside boardslide skateboard tutorial

name: Noseslide
history: The nose of the board slides along the obstacle while the tail is elevated. A very stylish and technical ledge trick.

name: Tailslide
history: The tail of the board slides on the obstacle while the nose is elevated. Can be done frontside or backside.

name: Lipslide
history: The board slides over the obstacle with the tail leading (opposite of a boardslide approach). The skater ollies over the obstacle and comes down on it sideways.

name: Frontside Lipslide
history: A lipslide approached from the frontside, requiring the skater to ollie over the rail/ledge frontside.
youtube_query: how to frontside lipslide skateboard tutorial

name: Backside Lipslide
history: A lipslide approached from the backside. The skater must ollie over the rail and come down on it from behind.
youtube_query: how to backside lipslide skateboard tutorial

name: Darkslide
history: Invented by Rodney Mullen, the board is flipped upside down so the grip tape slides along the obstacle. One of the most iconic and technical slide tricks ever created.

name: Gazelle Grind
history: A 540-degree body rotation combined with a grind on an obstacle. An extremely rare and advanced combination trick.

name: Switch Crooked Grind
history: A crooked grind performed in switch stance. Adds significant difficulty to an already technical grind.

name: Nollie Crooked Grind
history: A crooked grind entered from nollie stance. The nose is popped to enter the grind.

name: Switch 50-50
history: A 50-50 grind performed in switch stance. Both trucks grind while the skater rides in their non-dominant direction.

name: Switch Nosegrind
history: A nosegrind in switch stance. The front truck (now the weaker foot's side) grinds on the obstacle.

name: Switch Smith Grind
history: A Smith grind performed in switch stance. A significantly advanced version of the already technical Smith grind.

name: Switch Feeble Grind
history: A feeble grind in switch stance. The back truck (weaker side) hangs on the far side of the rail while grinding.

name: Kickflip 50-50
history: A kickflip into a 50-50 grind. The board flips before landing in the grind position. A popular trick in tech street skating.

name: Heelflip Nosegrind
history: A heelflip that lands into a nosegrind. Requires precise flick and catch to enter the grind cleanly.

name: Kickflip Nosegrind
history: A kickflip entry into a nosegrind. A technical ledge trick combining a flip trick with the precision of the nosegrind.

name: Noseblunt Slide
history: The nose of the board digs into the top of the obstacle while sliding. An extremely technical ledge trick requiring perfect approach.
youtube_query: how to noseblunt slide skateboard tutorial

name: Blunt Slide
history: The tail of the board slides on the obstacle while stalled at an angle. A classic ledge trick combining the blunt stall with forward momentum.
youtube_query: how to blunt slide skateboard tutorial

name: Heelflip Boardslide
history: A heelflip into a boardslide position. Requires the board to be caught sideways after the heel flip rotation.

name: Kickflip Boardslide
history: A kickflip into a boardslide. One of the classic tech ledge tricks.

name: Hardflip Nosegrind
history: A hardflip into a nosegrind. An extremely technical combination rarely attempted.

name: Frontside Noseslide
history: A noseslide entered from the frontside. The skater's chest faces the obstacle during the slide.
youtube_query: how to frontside noseslide skateboard tutorial

name: Backside Noseslide
history: A noseslide entered from the backside. The skater's back faces the obstacle during the slide.
youtube_query: how to backside noseslide skateboard tutorial

name: Frontside Tailslide
history: A tailslide where the approach is frontside. A highly stylish trick when done on long ledges.
youtube_query: how to frontside tailslide skateboard tutorial

name: Backside Tailslide
history: A tailslide approached from the backside. Considered more difficult due to the blind commitment required.
youtube_query: how to backside tailslide skateboard tutorial

name: Nosegrind to Tailslide
history: A nosegrind that transitions into a tailslide mid-obstacle. An advanced ledge line trick requiring body repositioning during the trick.

name: 5-0 to Fakie
history: A 5-0 grind that exits by rolling backward in fakie. A smooth and stylish exit from a grind used on ledges and curbs.

name: Nosegrind to Fakie
history: A nosegrind that exits rolling fakie. A clean technical finish to a nosegrind.

name: Boardslide to Fakie
history: A boardslide that exits fakie (rolling backward). A popular slide exit when going from the middle of a rail.

name: Hurricane Grind
history: The back truck grinds backside while the nose pivots around and the front truck swings out to the backside. A classic grind combination.
youtube_query: how to hurricane grind skateboard tutorial

name: Frontside Hurricane
history: A hurricane grind entered from the frontside. Less common than the backside version and requires different approach mechanics.

name: Pivot Fakie
history: The tail stalls on an obstacle and the board pivots 180 degrees to exit fakie. A foundational trick in both street and transition skating.
youtube_query: how to pivot fakie skateboard tutorial

name: Nosepick
history: The nose of the board locks on the corner of an obstacle while the back end is lifted. A stylish stall trick.

name: Tailpick
history: The tail picks on the corner of an obstacle while the nose is elevated. The counterpart to the nosepick.

name: Switch Boardslide
history: A boardslide performed in switch stance. The approach and body mechanics are reversed compared to the regular boardslide.

name: Switch Noseslide
history: A noseslide in switch stance. A highly coveted tech slide trick.

name: Switch Tailslide
history: A tailslide performed in switch stance. Considered one of the harder switch slide tricks.

name: Nollie Noseslide
history: A noseslide entered from nollie stance. The nose is popped while approaching the obstacle.

name: Nollie Tailslide
history: A tailslide entered from nollie stance. The nollie pop launches the skater into the slide position.

name: Halfcab Noseslide
history: A half-cab (fakie backside 180) into a noseslide. A creative combination merging the spin entry with the technical slide.

name: Halfcab Boardslide
history: A half-cab entry into a boardslide. The 180-degree spin sets up the perpendicular slide position.

name: Kickflip Lipslide
history: A kickflip over the rail into a lipslide position. Requires the board to flip and then land across the obstacle.

name: Heelflip Lipslide
history: A heelflip into a lipslide. The heel flick must be timed perfectly to set up the slide position.

name: Nollie Boardslide
history: A boardslide entered from nollie stance. The nose is popped to launch into the perpendicular slide.

name: Fakie Boardslide
history: A boardslide entered while rolling fakie. The reverse approach creates unique spatial challenges.

name: 360 Nosegrind
history: A 360 rotation into a nosegrind. Either the body or board completes a full spin before locking into the grind.

name: 360 Flip Nosegrind
history: A 360 flip (tre flip) into a nosegrind. One of the most technical ledge tricks combining the iconic flip with a precise grind entry.

name: Tre Flip Crooked Grind
history: A 360 flip into a crooked grind. An extremely technical trick combining two very demanding tricks.

name: Wallie
history: The skater rides at a wall, presses their front truck or nose against it, and uses the wall to pop off and continue skating. An old-school-influenced trick.

name: Wallie Nosegrind
history: A wallie combined with a nosegrind off a wall or curb. A creative street trick seen in creative skating lines.

name: Manny Pad 50-50
history: A 50-50 grind on a manual pad, an obstacle specifically designed for manuals and grinds at low height. A staple of skatepark and street plaza skating.

name: Waxed Curb Slide
history: Any slide trick performed on a waxed concrete curb. Curb skating was foundational to street skating culture in the 1980s and 1990s.

name: Kinked Rail Boardslide
history: A boardslide on a rail with one or more kinks (changes in angle). The skater must adjust position mid-slide to follow the kink.

name: Handrail Nosegrind
history: A nosegrind on a handrail, typically on stairs. A highly technical and dangerous trick requiring precise lock-in.

name: Handrail 50-50
history: A 50-50 grind on a stair handrail. One of the classic rail tricks in street skating since the late 1980s.

name: Pole Jam
history: The skater rides up a pole or post at an angle to pop off it. A creative street trick used in lines with urban architecture.

name: Bench Noseslide
history: A noseslide on a park bench or similar ledge. Benches are classic street skating obstacles since the 1980s.
